# fix(kaizen-observability): retain async sink tasks against GC (M1)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `fbcdfa39ec9e` — fix(kaizen-observability): retain async sink tasks against GC (M1)

**Body**:

Security-reviewer flagged TraceExporter._run_async leaks coroutines.
loop.create_task returns a Task the event loop only weakly references;
if GC fires mid-coroutine, the task is cancelled and the sink write
is silently lost. Trace events disappear intermittently under load.

Fix:
  * self._pending_tasks: set[asyncio.Task] initialized at __init__
  * _run_async becomes an instance method (was @staticmethod); every
    scheduled task is added to the set and a done-callback discards
    it on completion (bounded retention window = 'in-flight tasks only')
  * new async aclose() method awaits all outstanding tasks via
    asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True). Exceptions from pending
    tasks are logged (WARN) not propagated — aclose is cleanup, not retry

3 Tier 1 tests cover:
  * async_sink_tasks_retained_against_gc — scheduled task anchored
    in _pending_tasks until aclose drains it
  * aclose_tolerates_sink_exceptions — a failing async sink does not
    cascade into the caller's shutdown path
  * aclose_on_empty_exporter_is_noop — no-pending fast-path

PR#6 of issue #567 — security-reviewer feedback on PR#587 (M1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
